title: Use Drizzle ORM on bun:sqlite for V1 durable state
rationale: SPEC §6 mandates bun:sqlite (WAL) for zero-dep, in-process state and Drizzle for type safety + multi-dialect future. Schema lives in src/db/schema.ts; drizzle-kit generates SQL migrations into src/db/migrations/, and runtime applies them on open via drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite/migrator. Repos wrap drizzle queries so the rest of the system speaks domain verbs (enqueue/markRunning/finalize/append/listPending) instead of column-level updates.

description: Drizzle bun-sqlite types don't pass tsc --noEmit out of the box: singlestore-core/columns and a few sqlite-core query-builder d.ts files reference missing 'mysql2' modules and have abstract members that aren't implemented in the published declarations.
resolution: Add 'skipLibCheck: true' to tsconfig.json compilerOptions. We still get full type safety on our own code; the lib errors are exclusively in vendored .d.ts files we don't author. Re-evaluate when drizzle ships a clean type pass.

description: SQLite columns declared with drizzle's mode: 'timestamp' store unix seconds (integer), so a Date written at, e.g. 17:23:53.250 round-trips as 17:23:53.000 — millisecond precision is silently lost.
resolution: When tests compare row equality, pass an explicit 'now' value floored to whole seconds: new Date(Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) * 1000). For sub-second precision use mode: 'timestamp_ms' (not adopted in V1 — SPEC §10 chose seconds).

content: Drizzle's bun-sqlite .run() returns void (BaseSQLiteDatabase<'sync', void, TSchema>), not a result with .changes — callers needing affected-row counts must SELECT first and throw NotFoundError on missing, then DELETE/UPDATE.

name: startup-recovery-sweep-order
description: runStartupRecovery() must call repos.messages.resetDeliveredOrphans() BEFORE repos.runs.failAllRunning(). The orphan predicate keys off runs.state IN ('queued','running') — i.e. messages whose target run is non-terminal — and failAllRunning flips every running row to 'failed' (terminal). Reversed order leaves crash-time deliveries stuck because, by the time the message sweep runs, their target runs are already terminal and look like a normal completed delivery. SPEC §10.2 lays this out but doesn't constrain order; this is the operational ordering.

name: src/db layout (V1)
description: src/db/schema.ts (5 tables: burrows/runs/events/messages/meta + indices), src/db/client.ts (openDatabase: mkdir, WAL pragma, FK on, busy_timeout, drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite/migrator), src/db/migrations/{0000_init.sql,meta/_journal.json}, src/db/recovery.ts (runStartupRecovery), src/db/repos/{burrows,runs,events,messages,meta,index}.ts. createRepos(db) bundles them. Public re-exports go through src/index.ts.

content: Events are appended via EventsRepo.append() which assigns a per-burrow monotonic seq inside a SQLite transaction (SELECT MAX(seq) WHERE burrow_id=?, then INSERT). The autoincrement events.id is global; events.seq is per-burrow. Don't insert into events directly — go through the repo or seq drift is silent.
